mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate above 4.25% signals monetary tightening that increases discount rates on equity valuations. Higher FEDFUNDS directly compresses forward S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through reduced present value of future corporate earnings.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.25%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month decline in PAYEMS (total nonfarm payroll employment) signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative PAYEMS signal, or correlation between PAYEMS growth and subsequent 60-day SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) below 1900k units signals weakening residential demand that cascades into reduced construction employment, materials demand, and consumer wealth effects. This demand contraction produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as forward-looking equity markets price in demand destruction.
regime_hint: housing cycle peak with demand deterioration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances when HOUST < 1900k, or rolling 1-year correlation between HOUST and 5-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above 110.0 index level combined with rising PAYEMS signals robust supply-side capacity utilization and labor demand. This dual expansion mechanism produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through earnings growth expectations and margin expansion.
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with strong supply capacity
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when both INDPRO > 110.0 and PAYEMS growth > 0.1%, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) declining month-over-month signals consumer demand weakness that reduces corporate revenue expectations. This demand signal produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as equity markets reprrice earnings forecasts downward.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative RSAFS signal, or Spearman rank correlation between RSAFS growth and 60-day SPX returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.12).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 4.8% signals labor market slack that reduces wage pressure and increases recession probability. This labor market deterioration produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through both lower earnings growth expectations and higher equity risk premiums.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances when UNRATE > 4.8%, or rolling 1-year Rank IC between UNRATE and 20-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) rising above 198.5 index level combined with FEDFUNDS above 4.2% signals sustained monetary tightening that constrains equity valuations through both higher discount rates and earnings margin compression from input cost inflation. This dual monetary-inflation mechanism produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances when both CPIAUCSL > 198.5 and FEDFUNDS > 4.2%, or interaction coefficient between inflation and rates is not statistically significant (p > 0.12) in multivariate model.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) below 11250 billion chained 2017 dollars signals economic deceleration that reduces corporate earnings growth expectations. This demand-side slowdown produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as forward-looking equity markets anticipate lower earnings revisions.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances when GDPC1 < 11250, or correlation between GDPC1 level and subsequent 20-day SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.15) over any 3-year rolling window.
